provenance.generation: deterministic local generation from trusted guideline ranges and recommended ICU dataset references

guardrailsApplied.tidalVolume: Runtime recommendation logic calculates patient-specific tidal volume from current patient reference weight and never falls back to matched-case weight.
guardrailsApplied.pressureSupport: Seed pressure-support values capped at low-assist levels for comfortable spontaneous breathing and at bounded rescue levels otherwise.

schema.notes: Ventilation recommendation seed for initial decision-support suggestions only. Values are not autonomous orders.

observationModel.timeSeries: optional repeated vital sign or ventilator observations

intendedUse.warning: Suggested ventilation settings are decision support only and are not clinical orders.
intendedUse.validationRequirement: A qualified clinician must confirm, adjust, or reject every suggested value before use.

sources.citation: ATS/ESICM/SCCM clinical practice guideline: mechanical ventilation in adult patients with ARDS. Lower tidal volumes 4-8 mL/kg PBW and plateau pressure below 30 cmH2O.
Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021: low tidal volume ventilation for sepsis-induced ARDS and plateau pressure upper limit of 30 cmH2O.
PALICC-2 pediatric ARDS guidance summarized as physiologic tidal volumes around 6-8 mL/kg, lower volumes when pressure limits require, and plateau pressure limits around 28 cmH2O.
sources.publisher: Pediatric Acute Lung Injury Consensus Conference

sources.citation: Neonatal volume-targeted ventilation literature commonly uses lower tidal volume targets around 4-6 mL/kg with pathway-specific review.
MIMIC-IV is a large deidentified emergency department and ICU dataset on PhysioNet, suitable for future credentialed validation but not copied into this seed.
eICU Collaborative Research Database is a multicenter deidentified ICU database on PhysioNet, suitable for future credentialed validation but not copied into this seed.
AI Vent development clinician-approved seed guardrails derived from lung-protective ventilation principles; replace with facility governance-approved protocol before production clinical use.
AHRQ coordinated SAT/SBT protocol: SBT safety screen uses oxygenation and spontaneous effort criteria; pressure support ventilation under 7 cmH2O or CPAP 5 cmH2O is used for trials.
sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
